changes.motivation: Root package.json had unpinned (^) dependency versions for ajv and yaml that didn't match pinned versions in packages/website and packages/cli, causing a pre-commit hook failure on package.json consistency checks.
changes.summary: Fixed root package.json to pin ajv to 8.18.0 and yaml to 2.7.1, matching the exact versions used in the workspace packages.

changes.motivation: 77 git commits since the last edge release had no changelog coverage and needed to be reviewed for user-facing impact.
changes.summary: Processed 77 unvalidated commits through changelog-commit-reviewer subagents: created 54 new entries, updated 5 existing entries, and skipped 18 internal-only commits. All commits marked as validated in review.yaml.

changes.motivation: Two todo items in review.yaml flagged corrected component names (lint→pf and lint→git) that needed human confirmation before removal.
changes.summary: Reviewed and removed both resolved todos from review.yaml after confirming the component corrections were accurate.

changes.motivation: Two separate changelog entries both described CLI Nix build permission fixes introduced in adjacent commits, creating redundant coverage.
changes.summary: Condensed changelog from 68 to 67 entries by merging the two CLI Nix build permission fix entries into a single consolidated entry.

changes.motivation: The changelog lacked a top-level human-readable overview of the release themes for this development cycle.
changes.summary: Generated a top-level summary and 9 highlights for the changelog covering major themes: new kube_temporal module, SSO automation improvements, EKS bootstrap reliability, CLI enhancements, and AWS Service Quotas management.

changes.motivation: Individual changelog entries needed quality review to ensure accurate type classification, proper backtick formatting, complete impact lists, and relevant references.
changes.summary: Ran EnhanceEntry quality review on all 64 entries across 14 parallel batches. Removed 3 entries as non-user-facing internal tooling (bun2nix conditional, dependency pinning CI, ESLint upgrade). Reclassified aws_organization AWS provider v6 from update to breaking_change and EC2 vCPU quota check from improvement to addition. Fixed 11 spurious CLI impacts that were attached to the wrong entry (kube_argo memory fix vs PanfactumLightCommand refactor). Added backtick formatting, references, and expanded descriptions throughout.

changes.motivation: The aws_organization module's AWS provider v6 upgrade was reclassified to breaking_change during quality review, requiring upgrade instructions that weren't generated in the initial pass.
changes.summary: Created packages/website/src/content/changelog/main/upgrade.mdx with migration instructions for the aws_organization AWS provider v6 breaking change. Updated the corresponding log.yaml entry to reference the upgrade file.

problems: Root package.json used caret (^) prefix on ajv and yaml versions, making them unpinned, while packages/website and packages/cli used exact pinned versions. The inconsistency was only discovered when the pre-commit 'Check Package JSON Consistency' hook ran, not during initial changelog work. The root package.json dependency versions were also wrong (yaml: ^2.8.3 vs 2.7.1 in other packages), meaning they couldn't simply be unpinned — they needed to be corrected to match. There was no indication in the file or nearby docs explaining the pinning requirement or version source of truth.

Two changelog entries had an invalid devshell component name 'lint' (not in the devshell schema). These had been auto-generated by a previous session and required both automated detection and todo items in review.yaml before a human could confirm the corrections (lint→pf and lint→git). The devshell component schema is apparently not enforced at entry-creation time, only discovered later during review.

Entry 5834f0e7 (kube_argo memory fix) accumulated 11 spurious CLI command impacts that belonged to entry bf22b964 (PanfactumLightCommand refactor). This misattribution was only caught during the EnhanceEntry quality review pass, not during initial commit processing. The two commits were reviewed sequentially by the same subagent pattern, suggesting the subagent may have confused context between adjacent entries or a copy-paste error occurred in the YAML.

The aws_organization AWS provider v6 entry was initially classified as 'update' rather than 'breaking_change', so the upgrade-instructions-required check passed and no upgrade.mdx was generated in Step 2. Only after EnhanceEntry quality review reclassified it did the upgrade instructions get generated. This meant the upgrade instructions workflow ran out-of-order (mid-Step 6 instead of after Step 2), requiring the upgrade check to be re-run partway through quality review rather than at the designated workflow step.
